title: PubNub secret keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: PubNub Secret Key detected. PubNub secret keys are highly privileged server-side credentials used for administrative operations, access management (PAM), message encryption/decryption, and grant/revoke operations. Exposing secret keys enables attackers to gain complete control over your PubNub application, including modifying access permissions, reading encrypted messages, impersonating any user, manipulating channel groups, and disrupting your entire real-time infrastructure. Revoke this key immediately from your PubNub Admin Portal and generate a new one. Store secret keys exclusively on secure servers, never in client-side code, and use environment variables or secure secret management systems (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, Azure Key Vault, etc.).

PubNub Secret Key detected in variable assignment. Secret keys provide administrative access to PubNub services including PAM operations, encryption key management, channel group manipulation, and complete account control. Exposing these credentials enables attackers to grant themselves unlimited access, decrypt secure communications, modify permissions for all users, and compromise your entire real-time messaging infrastructure. Immediately revoke the exposed key and generate a new one. Never use secret keys in client-side code. Implement secure server-side storage using environment variables or dedicated secret management services.
